description: VS Code extension that auto-inserts shebangs, chmod +x, and positions cursor when creating script files

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and build setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the extension project fully scaffolded so that all subsequent stories have a working build pipeline and package.json with settings/commands declared.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json has name 'autosh', displayName 'autosh', publisher, description, categories, repository, icon field, engines.vscode, activationEvents: ['onStartupFinished']

package.json contributes.configuration declares all 5 settings: autosh.shebangs (object, with defaults for .sh/.zsh/.fish/.py/.rb/.pl), autosh.templates (object, default {}), autosh.enabledExtensions (string[], default ['.sh']), autosh.showNotification (boolean, default true), autosh.showStatusBar (boolean, default true)
package.json contributes.commands declares autosh.addShebang with title 'Autosh: Add Shebang'
package.json has scripts: compile (esbuild), watch, check-types (tsc --noEmit), package (vsce package)
tsconfig.json targets ES2020, module commonjs, strict mode, outDir dist, rootDir src, excludes node_modules
esbuild.js bundles src/extension.ts to dist/extension.js as commonjs, externals: ['vscode'], minified in production
.vscode/launch.json has 'Extension' debug config launching extensionDevelopmentPath
.vscode/tasks.json has npm watch task
.vscodeignore excludes src/, node_modules/, .vscode/, tsconfig.json, esbuild.js, *.ts
.gitignore excludes node_modules/, dist/, *.vsix
Create a minimal src/extension.ts stub with empty activate/deactivate so the project compiles
npm install succeeds and npm run compile produces dist/extension.js
Typecheck passes (npm run check-types)

userStories.title: Config and status bar modules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the config reader (src/config.ts) and status bar indicator (src/statusbar.ts) modules so that the handler logic can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: src/config.ts exports getShebangForExtension(ext: string): string | undefined — reads autosh.shebangs[ext] from workspace.getConfiguration('autosh')
src/config.ts exports getTemplateForExtension(ext: string): string — reads autosh.templates[ext], returns '' if not defined
src/config.ts exports isExtensionEnabled(ext: string): boolean — checks if ext is in autosh.enabledExtensions array
src/config.ts exports getAllShebangs(): Record<string, string> — returns the full autosh.shebangs object for Quick Pick usage
Config functions read settings fresh each call (no caching) so changes take effect without restart
src/statusbar.ts exports a function that creates a StatusBarItem aligned to the right with text '$(check) autosh'
Status bar item is hidden by default, shown for 3 seconds when flash is called, then auto-hides
Status bar flash respects autosh.showStatusBar setting — if false, does nothing
Status bar item is added to context.subscriptions for proper disposal
Typecheck passes (npm run check-types)

userStories.title: Auto-setup handler and extension entry point
userStories.description: As a developer, I want new script files to automatically get a shebang, chmod +x, and cursor positioned so I can start coding immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: src/handlers.ts exports handleFileCreated(uri, statusBar) that processes a single file URI
Handler extracts file extension via path.extname and checks isExtensionEnabled — returns early if not enabled
Handler opens the document via workspace.openTextDocument and checks if it is empty (0 characters) — returns early if not empty

Handler builds content: shebang + '

' + template (if configured) + '
' — using getShebangForExtension and getTemplateForExtension
Content is inserted at position (0,0) via WorkspaceEdit and workspace.applyEdit
Document is saved after edit via document.save()
chmod 0o755 is applied via fs.promises.chmod on non-Windows platforms (process.platform !== 'win32'), wrapped in try/catch that logs warning on failure
File is opened in editor via window.showTextDocument, cursor positioned on first empty line after inserted content
Cursor position is visible via editor.revealRange
If autosh.showNotification is true, shows information message 'autosh: shebang added + chmod +x'
Status bar flash is triggered after successful setup
src/extension.ts activate() registers workspace.onDidCreateFiles listener that calls handleFileCreated for each created file
src/extension.ts activate() creates the status bar item and registers the autosh.addShebang command (can be a stub for now)
All disposables pushed to context.subscriptions
deactivate() is an empty function
Typecheck passes (npm run check-types)

userStories.title: Manual 'Add Shebang' command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a command palette action to add a shebang to an existing file so I can fix files created before installing autosh.
acceptanceCriteria: src/handlers.ts exports handleAddShebangCommand(statusBar) registered as autosh.addShebang in extension.ts
If no active text editor, shows error message 'No active editor' and returns
If file already starts with '#!' (first line), shows warning 'File already has a shebang' and returns
If file extension is recognized (has entry in autosh.shebangs), uses that shebang
If file extension is unrecognized, shows Quick Pick with all available shebangs from getAllShebangs() for user to choose — items show extension and shebang string
If user cancels Quick Pick, returns without action
After determining shebang, inserts shebang + '\n\n' + template (if any) at position (0,0) via WorkspaceEdit
Applies chmod +x (same logic as auto handler — skip Windows, try/catch)
Positions cursor on first empty line after inserted content
Shows notification and flashes status bar (respecting settings)
Typecheck passes (npm run check-types)

userStories.title: README for marketplace
userStories.description: As a developer publishing autosh, I need a README with feature overview, configuration table, and usage examples for the VS Code Marketplace listing.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists at project root
Has a title, short description, and feature list (auto-shebang, auto-chmod, cursor positioning, configurable, manual command, status bar)
Has a 'Configuration' section with a table of all 5 autosh.* settings showing setting name, type, default, and description
Has a 'Usage' section explaining automatic behavior on file creation and the manual command
Has a 'Supported Extensions' section listing .sh, .zsh, .fish, .py, .rb, .pl with their default shebangs
Has a 'Template Example' section showing how to configure autosh.templates for set -euo pipefail
Typecheck passes (npm run check-types)
